what: 1. Setting Your Token using User Environment Variables (preferred)
title: Tokens
text: may find want reuse token, may several projects, convenient way store tokens separate location. One way personal .Renviron file. , check always confirm location file make sure ’s part cloud storage git GitHub. can use usethis::edit_r_environ() semi-permanently save token. time launch R session file run token visible specifically called Sys.getenv("REDCAPSYNC_FIRST_PROJECT"). Open .Renviron file usethis::edit_r_environ() Add token like … REDCAPSYNC_FIRST_PROJECT = "faKeTokeN" Save file Close Restart R Session (Session tab .rs.restartR()). Confirm Sys.getenv("REDCAPSYNC_FIRST_PROJECT") reference token ever made saved REDCapSync name. example, REDCap project object token name project$token_name. future, may set token different way.
code: #Install usethis if you don't have it. #install.packages("usethis")  usethis::edit_r_environ() # Now save your token.... (without the comment symbol '#') # REDCAPSYNC_FIRST_PROJECT = "faKeTokeN" # Save the file and Close # Restart R Session (session tab) # .rs.restartR() # this will also restart R session for you. Sys.getenv("REDCAPSYNC_FIRST_PROJECT") # now should contain your token

what: 2. Setting Your Token using keyring package
title: Tokens
text: default REDCapSync use keyring = NULL, OS system default typically unlocked default logged . can change default configuration give fine-tuning keyrings using configuration settings…
code: project <- load_project("TEST_CLASSIC")  project$set_keyring_token() # now enter token in pop-up # internally the package checks ... #1. Sys.getenv() token <- Sys.getenv(project$.internal$token_name) #2. followed by keyring token <- keyring::key_get(service = config$keyring.service(),                           username = project$project_name,                           keyring = config$keyring()) # you could set with the following; not preferred because token in script # if you do this set in securely in file that no one can ever see; never github! keyring::key_set_with_value(service = config$keyring.service(),                             username = project$project_name,                             password = "VeryNOTsecureWayToSetYourTOKEN",                             keyring = config$keyring()) options(redcapsync.config.keyring = "REDCapSync") # with this kind of method you could define multiple tokens in a private/secret # .R file which can be sourced to set all tokens in akeyring. Can also be  # locked and unlocked (see keyring webstite for details)

Automate common tasks cleaning, deidentification merges. Automate distribution user-defined Excel datasets local/cloud storage one many REDCap projects. Convert uncoded REDCap data R Excel upload using REDCap API. Power companion shiny app RosyREDCap leveraging combined strengths R REDCap, users can maintain strong data pipelines include statistics, visuals, even shiny applications!

text: Wraps dozens 'REDCap' API endpoints standardized R6 object. Research Electronic Data Capture ('REDCap') survey database web application software maintained Vanderbilt University. robust application programming interface (API) utilized several R packages. 'REDCapSync' uses 'redcapAPI' 'REDCapR' behind--scenes retrieve metadata, data, log details project. minimize unnecessary server calls, interim 'REDCap' log analyzed used update necessary records. Furthermore, user can define custom datasets save directory. datasets continue refresh projects synced. secure, standardized, API-efficient, project-agnostic R object 'REDCap' projects, streamlines downstream use scripts, functions, shiny applications.

title: Manage REDCapSync projects — projects
text: projects central interface managing REDCap projects REDCapSync. provides convenient, persistent registry projects can accessed across R sessions. time project set synced, basic metadata stored locally projects can reloaded updated without reconfiguration.

title: Manage REDCapSync projects — projects
text: projects implemented singleton list object serves single entry point REDCapSync workflow. project-level operations—setup, loading, syncing, removal—accessed object. key advantage design support method chaining, even without loading namespace. many project methods return project objects, can write concise, readable workflows operate sequence:
code: # load, sync, and generate dataset to global REDCapSync::projects$load("TEST_CLASSIC")$   sync()$   generate_dataset(envir = globalenv(),                    filter_field = "var_branching",                    filter_choices = "Yes")

what: Setup or Load REDCapSync Project — setup_project
title: Setup or Load REDCapSync Project — setup_project
text: Setup load REDCapSync project object. Prepares new REDCapSync project recording REDCap URI, token name, sync settings, optional data selection preferences.

title: Setup or Load REDCapSync Project — setup_project
code: setup_project(   project_name,   dir_path,   redcap_uri,   token_name = paste0("REDCAPSYNC_", project_name),   sync_frequency = "daily",   labelled = TRUE,   get_type = "identified",   records = NA,   fields = NA,   forms = NA,   events = NA,   filter_logic = NA,   get_users = TRUE,   get_data = TRUE,   batch_size_download = 1000L,   batch_size_upload = 500L,   get_entire_log = FALSE,   log_days = 10L,   log_drop_details = FALSE,   log_drop_exports = FALSE,   get_files = FALSE,   get_file_repository = FALSE,   original_file_names = FALSE,   add_default_datasets = TRUE,   timezone = Sys.timezone(),   hard_reset = FALSE )  load_project(project_name)

title: Setup or Load REDCapSync Project — setup_project
text: project_name Character scalar. Unique uppercase project name spaces symbols. dir_path Optional character scalar. Directory REDCap project files stored. omitted, project available current R session persisted disk. redcap_uri Character scalar. Base URL REDCap API endpoint. token_name Optional character scalar. Environment variable name used store REDCap token. Defaults REDCAPSYNC_<project_name>. sync_frequency Character scalar. Allowed values "always", "hourly", "daily", "weekly", "monthly", "", "never". setting controls often future syncs considered due. labelled Logical scalar. TRUE, data preserved labelled values. Default TRUE. get_type Character scalar. REDCap API export type. One "identified", "deidentified", "deidentified_strict", "deidentified_super_strict". Default "identified". records Optional character vector record IDs request. fields Optional character vector field names request. forms Optional character vector form names request. events Optional character vector event names request. filter_logic Optional character scalar. REDCap filter logic used limit returned records record-events. get_users Logical scalar. TRUE, project configured retrieve REDCap users sync. get_data Logical scalar. TRUE, project configured retrieve REDCap data sync. batch_size_download Integer scalar. Number records request per download batch. Default 1000. batch_size_upload Integer scalar. Number records process per upload batch. Default 500. get_entire_log Logical scalar. TRUE, REDCap activity logs retrieved full. Default FALSE. log_days Integer scalar. Number days log history consider new project set hard reset occurs. Default 10. log_drop_details Logical scalar. TRUE, detailed log export records excluded. log_drop_exports Logical scalar. TRUE, log export records excluded. get_files Logical scalar. TRUE, file attachments configured retrieved REDCap. Default FALSE. get_file_repository Logical scalar. TRUE, file repository configured retrieved REDCap. Default FALSE. original_file_names Logical scalar. TRUE, retrieved files keep original names. Default FALSE. add_default_datasets Logical scalar. TRUE, dataset templates added new project. Default TRUE. timezone Optional character scalar. Time zone used REDCap data timestamps. Defaults Sys.timezone(). hard_reset Logical scalar. TRUE, existing project project_name ignored fresh project object initialized. Default FALSE.
